variant_label: Foreign aid — Export-Import Bank loan (proposed)
notes: Proposed EXIM Bank loan for road building and agricultural improvement; Iran had not yet accepted as of this NSC draft policy statement. [cite: FRUS 1952-1954, Iran, 1951-1954, Document 6: Draft Statement of Policy Proposed by the National Security Council, Washington, March 14, 1951, PDF p. 65 (printed p. 28)]

variant_label: Foreign aid — Export-Import Bank loan (pending, referenced)
notes: Same $25 million EXIM loan referenced as still pending in this NIE discussing Iran's likely course of action. [cite: FRUS 1952-1954, Iran, 1951-1954, Document 13: National Intelligence Estimate, Washington, April 5, 1951, PDF p. 97 (printed p. 60)]

variant_label: Foreign aid — Point Four aid offered to Iran (per Kashani account of Prime Minister's rejection)
notes: CIA station note recounting Kashani's account that he advised the Prime Minister to refuse the $23,000,000 Point Four aid offered by the US if any strings were attached. Kashani rhetorically contrasted this with "even $23 billion" of aid -- the billion figure is rhetorical hyperbole, not a real aid offer, preserved here only as reported speech. [cite: FRUS 1952-1954, Iran, 1951-1954, Document 61: Despatch From the Station in Iran to the Central Intelligence Agency, Tehran, January 10, 1952, PDF p. 209 (printed p. 172)]

variant_label: Foreign aid — Recent IMF drawing
notes: "8 million dollars recently acquired from the International Monetary Fund" cited as one of the government's stopgap financing sources. [cite: FRUS 1952-1954, Iran, 1951-1954, Document 63: National Intelligence Estimate (NIE-46), "Probable Developments in Iran in 1952 in the Absence of an Oil Settlement", Washington, February 4, 1952, PDF p. 216 (printed p. 179)]

variant_label: Foreign aid — US immediate grant proposed in joint US-UK oil-settlement Aide-Memoire
notes: On July 31, 1952 the Secretary of State handed the British Ambassador an Aide-Memoire proposing (1) an immediate US grant of $10 million to the Iranian Government as part of a joint approach to settle the oil dispute. [cite: FRUS 1952-1954, Iran, 1951-1954, Document 121: Memorandum From the Assistant Secretary of State for Near Eastern, South Asian and African Affairs (footnote referencing the July 31, 1952 Aide-Memoire, printed as Document 194 in the companion FRUS vol. X), Washington, September 10, 1952 (Aide-Memoire itself dated July 31, 1952), PDF p. 375 (printed p. 338)]

variant_label: Foreign aid — EXIM Bank loan probable if oil sales resume (repeat reference)
notes: Reiterates that the Export-Import Bank will probably complete arrangements for a $25 million loan if there is hope of resumed oil sales; Iran might also draw a "sizeable sum" from the IMF. [cite: FRUS 1952-1954, Iran, 1951-1954, Document 121: Memorandum From the Assistant Secretary of State for Near Eastern, South Asian and African Affairs (footnote referencing the July 31, 1952 Aide-Memoire, printed as Document 194 in the companion FRUS vol. X), Washington, September 10, 1952 (Aide-Memoire itself dated July 31, 1952), PDF p. 375 (printed p. 338)]

variant_label: Foreign aid — MDAP (military aid) program, present level
notes: Present level of Mutual Defense Assistance Program aid to Iran, cited alongside Point Four in a discussion of whether to increase either given Iran's instability. [cite: FRUS 1952-1954, Iran, 1951-1954, Document 179: Memorandum for the Record, Washington, March 18, 1953, PDF p. 537 (printed p. 500)]

variant_label: Foreign aid — Point Four program, present level
notes: Present level of the Point Four technical-assistance program, paired with the MDAP figure above ($66.6m and $20.8m respectively). [cite: FRUS 1952-1954, Iran, 1951-1954, Document 179: Memorandum for the Record, Washington, March 18, 1953, PDF p. 537 (printed p. 500)]

variant_label: Foreign aid — State Dept-recommended contingency fund for possible direct financial assistance
notes: Department of State recommended a $45 million contingency fund be set aside for possible direct financial assistance to the National Front Government; under Bureau of the Budget consideration as of this memo. [cite: FRUS 1952-1954, Iran, 1951-1954, Document 179: Memorandum for the Record, Washington, March 18, 1953, PDF p. 537 (printed p. 500)]

variant_label: Foreign aid — Proposed FY1954 technical assistance program level (Henderson's floor estimate)
notes: Henderson's telegram warns that if Congress approves only ~$10 million in technical assistance for 1954 without at least twice as much in supporting economic aid, TCI's program should be withdrawn entirely as ineffective. [cite: FRUS 1952-1954, Iran, 1951-1954, Document 198: Telegram From the Embassy in Iran to the Department of State, Tehran, April 30, 1953, PDF p. 582 (printed p. 545)]

variant_label: Foreign aid — Proposed FY1954 supporting economic aid (Henderson's recommended minimum multiple)
notes: Recommended minimum supporting economic aid: at least twice the ~$10 million technical assistance figure, i.e. ~$20 million, or the technical program should be withdrawn. [cite: FRUS 1952-1954, Iran, 1951-1954, Document 198: Telegram From the Embassy in Iran to the Department of State, Tehran, April 30, 1953, PDF p. 582 (printed p. 545)]

variant_label: Foreign aid — Proposed addition to FY1954 TCA/economic aid programs
notes: Dulles and Stassen, per this DMS-State conversation, concluded Iran's agrarian economy should be kept afloat with perhaps $15 or $20 million added to the presently-proposed TCA and economic aid programs for FY1954. [cite: FRUS 1952-1954, Iran, 1951-1954, Document 215: Memorandum of Conversation, "Economic Aid to Iran", Washington, June 2, 1953, PDF p. 619 (printed p. 582)]

variant_label: Foreign aid — TCI Village Council program budget
notes: Ambassador Henderson recalled having advised against TCI's $3,400,000 Village Council program, though too late to stop by this point. [cite: FRUS 1952-1954, Iran, 1951-1954, Document 216: Memorandum of Conversation, Washington, June 6, 1953, PDF p. 622 (printed p. 585)]

variant_label: Foreign aid — EXIM Bank loan for agricultural/road-building machinery (pending since mid-1950)
notes: Long-pending Export-Import Bank loan for agricultural and road-building machinery, originally proposed mid-1950, still under policy review as of June 1953. [cite: FRUS 1952-1954, Iran, 1951-1954, Document 218: Memorandum From the Deputy Assistant Secretary of State for Near Eastern, South Asian and African Affairs (GTI recommendations memo), Washington, June 15, 1953, PDF p. 628 (printed p. 591)]

variant_label: Foreign aid — Proposed FY1954 economic aid increase per Stassen/DMS understanding
notes: Mr. Stassen instructed DMS to open conversations with State on the understanding that US economic aid to Iran was to be increased by approximately $15,000,000 in FY 1954. [cite: FRUS 1952-1954, Iran, 1951-1954, Document 218: Memorandum From the Deputy Assistant Secretary of State for Near Eastern, South Asian and African Affairs (GTI recommendations memo), Washington, June 15, 1953, PDF p. 628 (printed p. 591)]

variant_label: Foreign aid — Qashqai/Amini requested economic aid upon successful action
notes: Companion figure to the $5,000,000 operational cost above -- $250,000,000 of economic aid by loan or grant demanded upon completion of a successful anti-Mossadeq action. Station/Roosevelt assessment noted these figures were 'not necessarily firm... probably based on ignorance.' [cite: FRUS 1952-1954, Iran, 1951-1954, Document 255: Memorandum From the Chief of the Iran Branch, Near East and Africa Division, Washington, July 30, 1953, PDF p. 680 (printed p. 643)]

variant_label: Foreign aid — TCA program, planned reduced annual level
notes: Present plans called for reducing the TCA (technical assistance) program in Iran to $10 million per year. [cite: FRUS 1952-1954, Iran, 1951-1954, Document 256: Memorandum From the Officer in Charge of Iranian Affairs (Stutesman) to the Director, Office of Greek, Turkish and Iranian Affairs (Richards), "Measures Which the United States Government Might Take in Support of a Successor Government to Mosadeq", Washington, undated (placed between Documents 255 [July 30, 1953] and 259 [August 12, 1953]), PDF p. 684 (printed p. 647)]

variant_label: Foreign aid — TCA program, then-current expenditure rate
notes: Then-current TCA expenditure rate (covering FY1952 and FY1953 appropriations) was about $3 million a month, or $35 million a year. [cite: FRUS 1952-1954, Iran, 1951-1954, Document 256: Memorandum From the Officer in Charge of Iranian Affairs (Stutesman) to the Director, Office of Greek, Turkish and Iranian Affairs (Richards), "Measures Which the United States Government Might Take in Support of a Successor Government to Mosadeq", Washington, undated (placed between Documents 255 [July 30, 1953] and 259 [August 12, 1953]), PDF p. 684 (printed p. 647)]

variant_label: Foreign aid — TCA program, then-current expenditure rate (annualized)
notes: Annualized equivalent of the ~$3 million/month TCA expenditure rate cited in the same document. [cite: FRUS 1952-1954, Iran, 1951-1954, Document 256: Memorandum From the Officer in Charge of Iranian Affairs (Stutesman) to the Director, Office of Greek, Turkish and Iranian Affairs (Richards), "Measures Which the United States Government Might Take in Support of a Successor Government to Mosadeq", Washington, undated (placed between Documents 255 [July 30, 1953] and 259 [August 12, 1953]), PDF p. 684 (printed p. 647)]

variant_label: Foreign aid — Proposed dam/irrigation development aid
notes: Recommended: the US should be positioned to offer at least $15 million immediately to help Iran complete dams or other long-planned development projects. [cite: FRUS 1952-1954, Iran, 1951-1954, Document 256: Memorandum From the Officer in Charge of Iranian Affairs (Stutesman) to the Director, Office of Greek, Turkish and Iranian Affairs (Richards), "Measures Which the United States Government Might Take in Support of a Successor Government to Mosadeq", Washington, undated (placed between Documents 255 [July 30, 1953] and 259 [August 12, 1953]), PDF p. 684 (printed p. 647)]

variant_label: Foreign aid — Cost per irrigation project (of three described in missing Appendix II)
notes: Appendix II (not found attached) reportedly described three separate irrigation projects, each developable with $45 million over a three-year period. [cite: FRUS 1952-1954, Iran, 1951-1954, Document 256: Memorandum From the Officer in Charge of Iranian Affairs (Stutesman) to the Director, Office of Greek, Turkish and Iranian Affairs (Richards), "Measures Which the United States Government Might Take in Support of a Successor Government to Mosadeq", Washington, undated (placed between Documents 255 [July 30, 1953] and 259 [August 12, 1953]), PDF p. 684 (printed p. 647)]

variant_label: Foreign aid — EXIM Bank development loan long earmarked for Iran
notes: The Export-Import Bank had long earmarked a $25 million loan for Iranian economic development, seen as advanceable quickly with Department encouragement. [cite: FRUS 1952-1954, Iran, 1951-1954, Document 256: Memorandum From the Officer in Charge of Iranian Affairs (Stutesman) to the Director, Office of Greek, Turkish and Iranian Affairs (Richards), "Measures Which the United States Government Might Take in Support of a Successor Government to Mosadeq", Washington, undated (placed between Documents 255 [July 30, 1953] and 259 [August 12, 1953]), PDF p. 684 (printed p. 647)]

variant_label: Foreign aid — Net foreign-aid requirement (optimistic scenario)
notes: After Central Bank financing, a net requirement of $45 million for aid from abroad remains under the optimistic scenario. [cite: FRUS 1952-1954, Iran, 1951-1954, Document 256: Same memorandum, Appendix I ("Iran's yearly budgetary deficit"), Washington, undated, PDF p. 687 (printed p. 650)]

variant_label: Foreign aid — US emergency economic assistance grant to Zahedi government
notes: The White House announced on September 5, 1953 that $45 million would be made available for immediate economic assistance to Iran, in addition to existing military and technical assistance programs -- the single most-cited aid figure across the post-coup documents in this volume. [cite: FRUS 1952-1954, Iran, 1951-1954, Document 301: Letter From Iranian Prime Minister Zahedi to President Eisenhower (editorial footnote reporting the White House announcement), Tehran, August 26, 1953 (announcement: Washington, September 5, 1953), PDF p. 752 (printed p. 715)]

variant_label: Foreign aid — Zahedi's view that emergency grant is insufficient
notes: Prime Minister Zahedi considered the $45,000,000 grant too small to undertake necessary development and job-creating projects and considered resigning before being reassured by Ambassador Henderson. [cite: FRUS 1952-1954, Iran, 1951-1954, Document 319: Briefing Notes Prepared in the Central Intelligence Agency for Director of Central Intelligence Dulles, Washington, undated (NSC Briefing, ca. September 17, 1953), PDF p. 797 (printed p. 760)]

variant_label: Foreign aid — Point IV director Warne's estimate of aid needed to cover 7-month budget deficit
notes: Point IV Director Warne estimated $37,000,000 of American aid was required to meet Iran's budget deficit for the next 7 months. [cite: FRUS 1952-1954, Iran, 1951-1954, Document 319: Briefing Notes Prepared in the Central Intelligence Agency for Director of Central Intelligence Dulles, Washington, undated (NSC Briefing, ca. September 17, 1953), PDF p. 797 (printed p. 760)]

variant_label: Foreign aid — Of which, remaining for economic development
notes: Of the $37,000,000 estimate, $8,000,000 would remain available for economic development after covering the 7-month deficit. [cite: FRUS 1952-1954, Iran, 1951-1954, Document 319: Briefing Notes Prepared in the Central Intelligence Agency for Director of Central Intelligence Dulles, Washington, undated (NSC Briefing, ca. September 17, 1953), PDF p. 797 (printed p. 760)]

variant_label: Foreign aid — US emergency aid received by Zahedi government (confirmed)
notes: CIA monthly report confirms the new Iranian government received emergency aid in the amount of $45 million from the U.S. [cite: FRUS 1952-1954, Iran, 1951-1954, Document 326: Monthly Report Prepared in Directorate of Plans, CIA -- "Iran, September 1953", Washington, undated (October 1953 monthly report), PDF p. 815 (printed p. 778)]

variant_label: Foreign aid — Remaining runway on emergency aid program (as of late Oct 1953)
notes: Ambassador stated the emergency aid program now under way had five more months to run; at the end of that time Iran must find some other source of revenue. [cite: FRUS 1952-1954, Iran, 1951-1954, Document 341: Despatch From the Embassy in Iran to the Department of State, Tehran, October 30, 1953, PDF p. 856 (printed p. 819)]

variant_label: Foreign aid — Emergency $45m grant projected exhaustion
notes: The $45 million emergency grant extended soon after Zahedi took office was projected to cover operating expenses until about February or March 1954. [cite: FRUS 1952-1954, Iran, 1951-1954, Document 347: National Intelligence Estimate, Washington, November 16, 1953, PDF p. 878 (printed p. 841)]

variant_label: Foreign aid — IMF drawing in first post-nationalization year
notes: The government also obtained $8.75 million from the International Monetary Fund during the first post-nationalization year. [cite: FRUS 1952-1954, Iran, 1951-1954, Document 347: National Intelligence Estimate, Washington, November 16, 1953, PDF p. 878 (printed p. 841)]

variant_label: Foreign aid — Zahedi government's committed development program
notes: The Zahedi Government committed to a development program calling for annual expenditure of 3.9 billion rials ($43 million) to raise living standards and reduce unemployment. [cite: FRUS 1952-1954, Iran, 1951-1954, Document 347: National Intelligence Estimate, Washington, November 16, 1953, PDF p. 878 (printed p. 841)]

variant_label: Foreign aid — Continuation of technical/economic aid program, annual rate
notes: Technical and economic assistance program continuing at approximately $23 million a year, cited repeatedly through this NIE as the baseline non-emergency aid level. [cite: FRUS 1952-1954, Iran, 1951-1954, Document 347: National Intelligence Estimate, Washington, November 16, 1953, PDF p. 878 (printed p. 841)]

variant_label: Foreign aid — Estimated required emergency economic assistance rate
notes: Emergency economic assistance at a rate of at least $50 to $55 million a year, alongside continued $23 million technical/economic aid, judged necessary to meet minimum budgetary and FX requirements. [cite: FRUS 1952-1954, Iran, 1951-1954, Document 347: National Intelligence Estimate, Washington, November 16, 1953, PDF p. 878 (printed p. 841)]

variant_label: Foreign aid — Possible additional margin needed in current US fiscal year
notes: An additional $10 to $15 million might have to be made available in the current US fiscal year beyond the base estimates, to cover political-pressure-driven expenditures. [cite: FRUS 1952-1954, Iran, 1951-1954, Document 347: National Intelligence Estimate, Washington, November 16, 1953, PDF p. 878 (printed p. 841)]

variant_label: Foreign aid — Near East/Africa regional contingency fund (FY1955 budget, not Iran-specific)
notes: A special contingency fund of $45 million for the WHOLE Near East and Africa region (not Iran alone) was included in the FY1955 budget, some of which might be available for Iran. [cite: FRUS 1952-1954, Iran, 1951-1954, Document 347: National Intelligence Estimate, Washington, November 16, 1953, PDF p. 878 (printed p. 841)]

variant_label: Foreign aid — Total US military aid to Iran programmed since 1950
notes: Since the military aid program began in 1950, a total of $101.4 million has been programmed for Iran. [cite: FRUS 1952-1954, Iran, 1951-1954, Document 347: National Intelligence Estimate, Washington, November 16, 1953, PDF p. 878 (printed p. 841)]

variant_label: Foreign aid — Total US military aid to Iran delivered since 1950
notes: Of the $101.4 million programmed, only $45.9 million had actually been delivered (value of end-items shipped plus packaging/handling/crating/transportation/training) as of this Nov 1953 NIE. [cite: FRUS 1952-1954, Iran, 1951-1954, Document 347: National Intelligence Estimate, Washington, November 16, 1953, PDF p. 878 (printed p. 841)]
